county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1845 from Marquette County in the Upper Peninsula. County seat is Houghton. Named for Douglass Houghton, Michigan state geologist who surveyed the copper region. Heart of the Copper Country.
county_metadata.research_tips: Houghton County Register of Deeds in Houghton holds land records from 1845. County Clerk maintains marriage records and court files. The Keweenaw copper mining district records are exceptionally rich -- Michigan Technological University Archives and Copper Country Historical Collections in Houghton hold mining company records, employment rolls, ethnic organization files, and photograph collections. The Calumet and Hecla Mining Company records are a treasure trove. Finnish American Heritage Center at Finlandia University has Finnish immigrant records. Houghton County attracted Finnish, Cornish, Italian, Croatian, and Slovenian miners. Church records (Finnish Lutheran, Catholic, Methodist) are essential. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. 1913 Italian Hall disaster records.

source_profiles.source_name: Houghton County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1845 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.source_name: Houghton County Probate Records -- Probate Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, guardianship records, and administration files from 1845. Michigan Probate Courts also handled adoption records, mental health commitments, and name changes.

source_profiles.source_name: Houghton County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1845. Michigan County Clerks issue marriage licenses. State registration of marriages began 1867. Pre-1867 marriages may also be documented in church records.

source_profiles.source_name: Houghton County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census for Houghton County from 1850 to 1950. Michigan territorial censuses (1827, 1834) may include early residents. Michigan state censuses (1874, 1884, 1894, 1904) provide intercensal data.

source_profiles.source_name: Houghton County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan is a public land state. Original federal land patents for Houghton County are available free online at BLM GLO. Cash entry, homestead, and military bounty land warrants. Essential for identifying first landowners.

source_profiles.source_name: Houghton County Mining Company Records
source_profiles.repository: Michigan Technological University Archives / Archives of Michigan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Employment rolls, payroll records, accident reports, and company town records from copper mining operations in Houghton County. Michigan Tech's Copper Country Historical Collections and the Archives of Michigan hold mining company records. Essential for tracing immigrant mining families.

county_metadata.record_losses: 1881 Great Thumb Fire destroyed courthouse and many records
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1859 from Sanilac County in the Thumb region. County seat is Bad Axe. Named for Lake Huron and the Huron people. 1881 Great Thumb Fire destroyed courthouse and many records (burned county).
county_metadata.research_tips: Huron County Register of Deeds in Bad Axe holds land records, but the 1881 Great Thumb Fire destroyed the courthouse and many early records. This is a burned county. Post-fire records restart from 1881. The Huron County Historical Society and Museum have reconstructed some records. German and Polish immigrants settled heavily in Huron County. Check the Archives of Michigan for surviving pre-fire records and BLM GLO for land patents. Catholic and Lutheran church records survived the fire in many cases and are essential substitutes. The 1881 fire devastated the entire Thumb region (Huron, Sanilac, Tuscola counties). FamilySearch microfilm covers post-fire vital records. Federal census records (1860, 1870) provide pre-fire population data. Port Austin and Caseville fishing community records.

source_profiles.source_name: Huron County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1881 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens. Some gaps due to 1881 Great Thumb Fire destroyed courthouse and many records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: 1881 Great Thumb Fire destroyed courthouse and many records

source_profiles.source_name: Huron County Probate Records -- Probate Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, guardianship records, and administration files from 1881. Michigan Probate Courts also handled adoption records, mental health commitments, and name changes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: 1881 Great Thumb Fire destroyed courthouse and many records

source_profiles.source_name: Huron County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1881. Michigan County Clerks issue marriage licenses. State registration of marriages began 1867. Pre-1867 marriages may also be documented in church records.

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan is a public land state. Original federal land patents for Huron County are available free online at BLM GLO. Cash entry, homestead, and military bounty land warrants. Essential for identifying first landowners.

source_profiles.source_name: Huron County Historical Newspapers -- Pre- and Post-1881 Fire
source_profiles.repository: Archives of Michigan / Library of Congress / Newspapers.com
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historical newspapers for Huron County. The 1881 Great Thumb Fire destroyed much of the Thumb region including courthouses. Newspapers from surrounding areas and post-fire editions document recovery. Michigan Digital Newspaper Archive and Chronicling America have digitized Michigan newspapers.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1838 from unorganized territory in central Lower Michigan. County seat is Mason. Named for Samuel D. Ingham, US Secretary of the Treasury. Contains Lansing (state capital since 1847) and East Lansing (Michigan State University).
county_metadata.research_tips: Ingham County Register of Deeds in Mason holds land records from 1838 organization. The county seat is Mason, but Lansing (the state capital since 1847) is also in Ingham County. County Clerk maintains marriage licenses and court records. Probate Court has estate files. The Capital Area District Library in Lansing has genealogical collections. The Library of Michigan in Lansing is a major genealogical resource with statewide coverage. Michigan State University in East Lansing has archives with local and state history. The Archives of Michigan (also in Lansing) holds state-level records. Oldsmobile/General Motors records for Lansing workers. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records and probate. BLM GLO land patents.

source_profiles.source_name: Ingham County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1838 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.source_name: Ingham County Probate Records -- Probate Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, guardianship records, and administration files from 1838. Michigan Probate Courts also handled adoption records, mental health commitments, and name changes.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1838. Michigan County Clerks issue marriage licenses. State registration of marriages began 1867. Pre-1867 marriages may also be documented in church records.

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records

source_profiles.repository: Walter P. Reuther Library (Wayne State University) / Archives of Michigan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Automobile manufacturing employment records, union (UAW) records, and industrial documentation for Ingham County. The Walter P. Reuther Library at Wayne State University holds United Auto Workers records and related labor archives. City directories and employee rosters trace worker families. Essential for 20th-century family research in Michigan's industrial counties.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Annual tax rolls listing property owners, assessed values, and personal property. Michigan tax rolls from 1838 at the Archives of Michigan. Essential for tracking land ownership and residency between census years.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historical newspapers for Ingham County including local weeklies and dailies. Obituaries, legal notices, marriage and death announcements, estate settlements. Michigan Digital Newspaper Archive and Chronicling America have digitized collections.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1837 from unorganized territory in west-central Lower Michigan. County seat is Ionia. Named for the Ionia region of ancient Greece. Agricultural county with state correctional facilities.
county_metadata.research_tips: Ionia County Register of Deeds in Ionia holds land records from 1837. County Clerk maintains marriage licenses and court records. Probate Court has estate files. The Blanchard House Museum and Historical Society in Ionia has genealogical materials. Early settlers came primarily from New York and New England. The Ionia Free Fair (est. 1855) is one of Michigan's oldest. The Ionia State Hospital (1885) records are partially at the Archives of Michigan. Strong Methodist and Baptist church records. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. BLM GLO land patents. Agricultural census records for this farming county are valuable for family research.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1837 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1857 from unorganized territory in northeast Lower Michigan. County seat is Tawas City. Named from a Schoolcraft coinage. Lake Huron shoreline county, lumber heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: Iosco County Register of Deeds in Tawas City holds land records from 1857 organization. County Clerk has marriage records and court files. The Iosco County Historical Museum has local genealogical materials. Lumber industry along the AuSable River and Lake Huron coast drove early settlement. East Tawas and Tawas City developed as lumber ports and later resort communities. German immigrants settled in significant numbers. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. BLM GLO land patents. Check the Archives of Michigan for lumber company records. The Tawas Bay area newspapers document local families.

source_profiles.repository: Find A Grave / Local historical and genealogical societies
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Iosco County churches, township, and family burial grounds. Find A Grave has growing online coverage. Local historical and genealogical societies have published cemetery surveys. Michigan Cemetery Source at the Library of Michigan is a statewide index.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1885 from Marquette and Menominee in the Upper Peninsula. County seat is Crystal Falls. Named for the iron ore deposits. Mining county with diverse immigrant communities.
county_metadata.research_tips: Iron County Register of Deeds in Crystal Falls holds land records from 1885 organization. County Clerk has marriage records and court files. The Iron County Museum in Caspian has an extensive genealogical collection focused on mining families and immigrant communities. Iron mining was the dominant industry. Finnish, Italian, Swedish, and Cornish immigrants formed the workforce. Check Marquette and Menominee counties for pre-1885 records. Michigan Technological University Archives has mining company records. Finnish Lutheran church records and Catholic parish records document immigrant families. Crystal Falls was an important county seat. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1885 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.repository: Michigan Technological University Archives / Archives of Michigan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Employment rolls, payroll records, accident reports, and company town records from iron mining operations in Iron County. Michigan Tech's Copper Country Historical Collections and the Archives of Michigan hold mining company records. Essential for tracing immigrant mining families.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1859 from unorganized territory in central Lower Michigan. County seat is Mt. Pleasant. Named for Queen Isabella of Spain. Home to Central Michigan University and the Isabella Indian Reservation (Saginaw Chippewa).
county_metadata.research_tips: Isabella County Register of Deeds in Mt. Pleasant holds land records from 1859. County Clerk maintains marriage records and court files. Probate Court has estate files. The Clarke Historical Library at Central Michigan University in Mt. Pleasant is an outstanding genealogical resource with Michigan and Great Lakes history collections. The Saginaw Chippewa Indian Tribe of Michigan (Isabella Reservation) has tribal enrollment and census records at the Ziibiwing Center of Anishinabe Culture and Lifeways. Treaty records at NARA document land cessions. German and Irish immigrants settled in the county. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. BLM GLO land patents.

source_profiles.source_name: Isabella County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1859 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1859. Michigan County Clerks issue marriage licenses. State registration of marriages began 1867. Pre-1867 marriages may also be documented in church records.

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan is a public land state. Original federal land patents for Isabella County are available free online at BLM GLO. Cash entry, homestead, and military bounty land warrants. Essential for identifying first landowners.

source_profiles.source_name: Ojibwe/Chippewa Tribal Records -- Isabella County Area
source_profiles.repository: NARA (Great Lakes Agency) / Tribal Government Offices / Archives of Michigan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Treaty records, tribal census rolls (annuity rolls), allotment records, and agency correspondence for Ojibwe/Chippewa communities in Isabella County. NARA holds Bureau of Indian Affairs records (Great Lakes Agency). Tribal government offices maintain enrollment records. Archives of Michigan has related territorial records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1832 from Washtenaw County in south-central Lower Michigan. County seat is Jackson. Named for Andrew Jackson. Birthplace of the Republican Party (1854). Home to Michigan State Prison (1839).
county_metadata.research_tips: Jackson County Register of Deeds in Jackson holds land records from 1832 organization. County Clerk maintains marriage licenses from 1833 and court records. Probate Court has estate files. The Jackson District Library Carnegie branch has a genealogy room. The Ella Sharp Museum has local history collections. Jackson was the birthplace of the Republican Party (1854, 'Under the Oaks'). Michigan State Prison in Jackson (opened 1839) has inmate records at the Archives of Michigan. The Underground Railroad had routes through Jackson County. Automobile industry records (Jackson was an early auto manufacturing center). FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. BLM GLO land patents.

source_profiles.source_name: Jackson County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1832 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.source_name: Jackson County Probate Records -- Probate Court

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan is a public land state. Original federal land patents for Jackson County are available free online at BLM GLO. Cash entry, homestead, and military bounty land warrants. Essential for identifying first landowners.

source_profiles.source_name: Underground Railroad and African American Records -- Jackson County
source_profiles.repository: Archives of Michigan / Burton Historical Collection (Detroit Public Library)
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan was a free state, but the Underground Railroad was active throughout southern Michigan with routes to Canada. Jackson County records include freedom seekers' documentation, abolitionist society records, African American church records, and community histories. The Burton Historical Collection and Archives of Michigan hold related materials.

source_profiles.repository: Walter P. Reuther Library (Wayne State University) / Archives of Michigan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Automobile manufacturing employment records, union (UAW) records, and industrial documentation for Jackson County. The Walter P. Reuther Library at Wayne State University holds United Auto Workers records and related labor archives. City directories and employee rosters trace worker families. Essential for 20th-century family research in Michigan's industrial counties.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Annual tax rolls listing property owners, assessed values, and personal property. Michigan tax rolls from 1832 at the Archives of Michigan. Essential for tracking land ownership and residency between census years.

source_profiles.repository: Archives of Michigan / Library of Congress / Newspapers.com
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historical newspapers for Jackson County including local weeklies and dailies. Obituaries, legal notices, marriage and death announcements, estate settlements. Michigan Digital Newspaper Archive and Chronicling America have digitized collections.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1830 from unorganized territory in southwest Lower Michigan. County seat is Kalamazoo. Named from a Potawatomi word. Home to Western Michigan University. Pharmaceutical and paper manufacturing center.
county_metadata.research_tips: Kalamazoo County Register of Deeds in Kalamazoo holds land records from 1830 organization. County Clerk maintains marriage licenses and court records. Probate Court has estate files. The Kalamazoo Public Library has an exceptional local history room with city directories, newspapers, and family files. Western Michigan University Archives and Regional History Collections hold extensive local and regional materials. The Kalamazoo Valley Museum has genealogical resources. Kalamazoo attracted Dutch settlers (Kalamazoo Dutch Reformed churches), African Americans (strong community from the 1840s), and later Southern and Eastern European immigrants. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. Archives of Michigan supplementary. Kalamazoo Gazette newspaper archives from 1835.

source_profiles.source_name: Kalamazoo County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1830 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.source_name: Kalamazoo County Probate Records -- Probate Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, guardianship records, and administration files from 1830. Michigan Probate Courts also handled adoption records, mental health commitments, and name changes.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census for Kalamazoo County from 1830 to 1950. Michigan territorial censuses (1827, 1834) may include early residents. Michigan state censuses (1874, 1884, 1894, 1904) provide intercensal data.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan is a public land state. Original federal land patents for Kalamazoo County are available free online at BLM GLO. Cash entry, homestead, and military bounty land warrants. Essential for identifying first landowners.

source_profiles.repository: Joint Archives of Holland / Calvin University Heritage Hall / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Reformed Church in America (RCA) and Christian Reformed Church (CRC) records from Dutch immigrant congregations. Baptisms, memberships, marriages, and death records. The Joint Archives of Holland (Hope College) and Calvin University Heritage Hall in Grand Rapids hold extensive Dutch Reformed church records. Essential for tracing Dutch immigrant families who settled in western Michigan from 1847.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1871 from unorganized territory in northwest Lower Michigan. County seat is Kalkaska. Named from a Schoolcraft coinage. Lumber heritage, later oil and gas development.
county_metadata.research_tips: Kalkaska County Register of Deeds in Kalkaska holds land records from 1871 organization. County Clerk has marriage records and court files. The Kalkaska County Historical Society has local genealogical materials including cemetery transcriptions. Lumber industry drove early settlement, followed by oil and gas exploration. The county was sparsely populated after timber was exhausted. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. BLM GLO land patents document original purchasers. Check the Archives of Michigan for lumber company records. The Kalkaska Leader newspaper archives document local families.
